name: 1737 Need to Talk
cost: Free
fit: Free call or text for anyone who needs to talk, wants support, or needs help finding the next service.
firstStep: Call or text 1737. You can simply say: I am not coping and need help choosing the next step.

name: Alcohol Drug Helpline
cost: Free
fit: Free, confidential alcohol and other drug support from trained counsellors, including help finding local treatment options.
firstStep: Call 0800 787 797, text 8681, or use webchat if local provider options do not fit yet.

name: Maori Alcohol Drug Helpline
cost: Free
fit: Free alcohol and drug support line for advice and referrals to kaupapa Maori services.
firstStep: Call 0800 787 798 and ask for alcohol or drug support, advice, or referral to kaupapa Maori services.

name: Pasifika Alcohol Drug Helpline
cost: Free
fit: Free alcohol and drug support line for advice and referrals to services developed for Pacific people.
firstStep: Call 0800 787 799 and ask for alcohol or drug support, advice, or a referral to Pasifika support.

name: Gambling Helpline
hours: 7 days a week; text available anytime
cost: Free
fit: Free gambling harm support for people worried about their own gambling or someone else's gambling.
firstStep: Call 0800 654 655, text 8006, or use the website chat/contact options.

name: Maori Gambling Helpline
hours: Specialist gambling support line
cost: Free
fit: Specialist gambling harm support line for Maori callers and whanau.
firstStep: Call 0800 654 656 and ask for gambling support for yourself or someone close to you.

name: Vai Lelei Pasifika Gambling Helpline
hours: Specialist gambling support line
cost: Free
fit: Specialist gambling harm support line for Pasifika callers, families, and communities.
firstStep: Call 0800 654 657 and ask for gambling support for yourself or someone close to you.

name: Meth Help
hours: Phone counselling service; contact for current callback times
cost: Free
fit: Free, confidential phone counselling for anyone in New Zealand concerned about methamphetamine use, including whanau and friends.
firstStep: Call 0800 METH HELP (0800 638 443) or email methhelp@odysseychch.org.nz.

name: Depression Helpline
cost: Free
fit: Free phone or text support from trained counsellors for depression, anxiety, and low mood.
firstStep: Call 0800 111 757 or text 4202 and say you want to talk about how you have been feeling.

name: Lifeline Aotearoa
hours: Check current hours
cost: Free
fit: Confidential mental health and distress support by phone or text.
firstStep: Call 0800 543 354 or text HELP to 4357.

name: OutLine Aotearoa
hours: Every evening 6pm-9pm
cost: Free support line and chat; counselling options may vary
fit: Free, confidential, all-ages Rainbow support line, online chat, and peer support for Rainbow, takatapui, questioning people, friends, and whanau.
firstStep: Call 0800 OUTLINE (0800 688 5463) between 6pm and 9pm, or use their online chat/support options.

name: Dr Christmas Seu
hours: Online assessments. Ask about current waitlist and appointment times.
cost: Initial 60 minute assessment listed from $700 on weekdays 8am-5pm; higher after-hours/weekend/public holiday pricing may apply.
fit: Psychiatry.nz telepsychiatry doctor. The site lists Dr Seu as a psychiatry registrar working under Dr Lim's supervision, with extensive psychiatry experience while working toward fellowship.
firstStep: Email refer@psychiatry.nz asking about referral requirements, waitlist, fees, supervision arrangements, and whether an online assessment is a good fit.

name: Dr Han Chung Lim
hours: Online assessments. Ask about current waitlist and appointment times.
cost: Initial 60 minute assessment listed from $700 on weekdays 8am-5pm; higher after-hours/weekend/public holiday pricing may apply.
fit: Psychiatry.nz telepsychiatry assessment provider for clients aged 16 and above. Dr Lim specialises in ADHD, depression, bipolar disorder, anxiety, OCD, PTSD, and psychosis.
firstStep: Email refer@psychiatry.nz asking about referral requirements, waitlist, fees, and whether an online assessment is a good fit.

name: Dr Helen Austin
hours: Appointment wait: Less than 1 month.
cost: Private specialist fees usually apply. Ask about referral needs, insurance, ACC, or any funded options.
fit: RANZCP Your Health in Mind psychiatrist listing. Services include Professional supervision. Patient groups listed include Doctors, psychiatrists or medical students. Works with Ages 18-60, Ages 60+.
firstStep: Call the practice asking about fit, referral requirements, fees, wait time, and the simplest booking step.

name: Dr Jimi MacMillan
hours: Online assessments. Ask about current waitlist and appointment times.
cost: Initial 60 minute assessment listed from $700 on weekdays 8am-5pm; higher after-hours/weekend/public holiday pricing may apply.
fit: Psychiatry.nz telepsychiatry doctor. The site lists Dr MacMillan (Ngapuhi) as a psychiatry registrar under Dr Lim's supervision, with experience across New Zealand and Australia and an interest in regional and rural mental health care.
firstStep: Email refer@psychiatry.nz asking about referral requirements, waitlist, fees, supervision arrangements, and whether an online assessment is a good fit.

name: Dr Staverton (Tony) Kautoke
hours: Online assessments. Ask about current waitlist and appointment times.
cost: Initial 60 minute assessment listed from $700 on weekdays 8am-5pm; higher after-hours/weekend/public holiday pricing may apply.
fit: Psychiatry.nz telepsychiatry doctor. The site lists Dr Kautoke as a medical officer of psychiatry working under Dr Lim's supervision, with extensive psychiatry experience while working toward fellowship.
firstStep: Email refer@psychiatry.nz asking about referral requirements, waitlist, fees, supervision arrangements, and whether an online assessment is a good fit.

name: Dr Vernon Reynolds
hours: Appointment wait: 1-3 months.
cost: Private specialist fees usually apply. Ask about referral needs, insurance, ACC, or any funded options.
fit: RANZCP Your Health in Mind psychiatrist listing. Special interests include Acceptance and commitment therapy, ADHD, Anxiety disorders, Autism spectrum disorders, Bipolar disorder, Cognitive behavioural therapy, Depression, Developmental disability. Services include Professional supervision, Shared care, After-hours appointments, Experience with interpreters, Home visits. Patient groups listed include Asylum seekers and refugees, Doctors, psychiatrists or medical students, Gender diverse, Gay and lesbian, Maori, Military and veterans, Non-English speaking, Physical disability. Works with Ages 1-17, Ages 18-60, Ages 16-24.
firstStep: Email the practice asking about fit, referral requirements, fees, wait time, and the simplest booking step.

hours: Ask the psychologist about availability and referral requirements
cost: Private fees vary; ask about ACC, EAP, insurance, or funded options

firstStep: Open the profile and send a short enquiry asking about availability, fees, telehealth, and whether they are a good fit for what is happening.

cost: Public/funded services may be available

firstStep: Call 0800 376 633, text 234, use webchat, or email a short message.

cost: Private fee listed on the practice site; ask about Southern Cross, WINZ Disability Allowance, ACC Sensitive Claims, EAP, or other funded options.
fit: Clinical psychology practice for adults. Listed focus includes depression, anxiety, panic, sleep problems, trauma, major life changes, ADHD and autism assessment, CBT, EMDR, and ACT.
firstStep: Email the practice with a short summary of what is happening and ask about availability, costs, funding options, and whether self-referral is enough.

name: Parker & Chin Psychology
address: Level 5, Westpac Building, 106 George Street, Dunedin 9016
hours: Ask the clinician or practice about availability, waitlists, and rates.
cost: Private fees vary by clinician; the practice FAQ says to ask about insurance or Disability Allowance funding.
fit: Dunedin-based clinical psychology practice. Listed services include psychological assessment and treatment, psychiatric consultation and review, neurodiversity assessments and treatment, ACC Sensitive Claims, and cultural supervision.
firstStep: Email the practice or a clinician from the team page asking about fit, current availability, rates, ACC or Disability Allowance options, and the simplest booking step.

name: DrewCrannitch Psychologists
address: The Exchange Collaboration Hub, Ground Floor, 205 Princes Street, Dunedin 9016
hours: Monday-Friday by appointment.
cost: Private fees may apply; ask about ACC Sensitive Claims, Visser & Associates, EAP, insurance, WINZ Disability Allowance, or funded options.
fit: Clinical psychology practice for young people and adults. Listed focus includes trauma, self-harm, suicidal behaviours, OCD, PTSD, depression, eating disorders, mood difficulties, generalised anxiety, health anxiety, ACC Sensitive Claims, youth/young adult support, and adult ADHD assessments.
firstStep: Email or call and ask about availability, whether Rachel or Sarah is the best fit, fees, ACC Sensitive Claims, and the simplest first appointment step.

name: University of Otago Clinical Psychology Centre
address: William James Building, 275 Leith Walk, North Dunedin, Dunedin 9016
hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm; appointments are usually weekly and arranged after referral screening.
cost: No charge for services at the Centre.
fit: No-charge training clinic supervised by registered clinical psychologists. Referrals can cover adults, children, anxiety disorders, OCD, depression, health behaviour problems, sleep issues, relationship difficulties, social skills, and some neuropsychological concerns. It does not accept self-referrals and declines some high-risk or severe presentations.
firstStep: Ask your GP, health professional, teacher, or support worker to refer you. You can email or call the Centre administrator first if you are unsure whether the referral is suitable.

name: Blue Harbour Mental Health
address: Princes Street Practice, Queens Building, Level 2, 109 Princes Street, Dunedin 9016
hours: The practice aims to respond to enquiries within two working days.
cost: Private specialist fees usually apply; ask about referral requirements, ACC Sensitive Claims, insurance, or funded options.
fit: Specialist psychiatry and psychotherapy practice in central Dunedin. Listed services include psychiatric consultations, treatment and follow-up, psychodynamic and psychoanalytic psychotherapy, and ACC Sensitive Claims assessment and treatment.
firstStep: Email with a short summary and ask about referral requirements, fees, availability, ACC Sensitive Claims, and whether psychiatry or psychotherapy is the better first appointment.

name: Ashburn Clinic Psychiatry
hours: Ask about outpatient availability and referral requirements.
cost: Private fees apply; the clinic says patients may be eligible for ACC or health insurance funding.
fit: Private psychiatric hospital and outpatient psychiatry service. Listed focus includes comprehensive psychiatric assessments and treatment, ADHD and ASD assessments, psychotherapy, medication management, and consideration of background, medical and social factors, alcohol and drugs, and medication.
firstStep: Call or email to ask about outpatient psychiatry availability, referral requirements, fees, ACC or insurance funding, and whether an assessment is the right first appointment.

hours: Emergency crisis contact

fit: Urgent mental health crisis support in Otago and Southland.
firstStep: Call 0800 467 846 and press 2 for Otago or 1 for Southland.
